docs.title: Mocky
docs.text:
Turn an idea or a Markdown outline into a polished PowerPoint deck — no Microsoft Office required.

Mocky runs the same on Windows, macOS, and Linux because it renders with python-pptx instead of automating Microsoft Office.

docs.title: What you get
docs.text:
• :material-lightbulb-on: Idea → deck. Scaffold a structured Markdown outline from a one-line idea using a template library.
  • :material-robot: AI-agent prompts. Generate ready-to-paste prompts for 15 agents/CLIs (Claude, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, and more).
  • :material-microsoft-powerpoint: Real .pptx. A cross-platform engine renders genuine PowerPoint files.
  • :material-package-variant: CLI and library. Use the mocky command or import mocky in your own code.
  • :material-docker: Container-ready. Ship it as a Docker image.

docs.title: Quick taste
docs.text:
from mocky import TemplateLibrary, MarkdownParser, PowerPointGenerator

markdown = TemplateLibrary.build_markdown("Launching Mocky 1.0", "product-launch")
path = TemplateLibrary.save_markdown(markdown, "generated_markdowns", "launch.md")

parsed = MarkdownParser().parse_markdown(path)
PowerPointGenerator().create_presentation(parsed["slides"], "presentations/launch.pptx")

Ready to try it? Head to Getting started.

Open source

Mocky is released under the MIT License. Contributions are welcome — see Contributing.

docs.title: AI agent prompts
docs.text: Mocky can generate ready-to-paste prompts for a range of AI agents and CLIs. It never calls those services itself — it produces prompt text you paste into the agent of your choice, so you stay in control of your own credentials.

docs.title: Supported agents
docs.text: Claude Code, Claude API, Anthropic API, Codex CLI, Cursor Agent, GitHub Copilot CLI, Gemini CLI, Gemini API, Grok, Qwen, Aider, Windsurf CLI, Trae CLI, Open Design, and Hermes — 15 in total.

docs.title: API
docs.text:
from mocky import AgentPromptBuilder

AgentPromptBuilder.list_agents()
# ['open design', 'windsurf cli', ..., 'anthropic api']

prompt = AgentPromptBuilder.build_prompt("claude code", "My idea", "product-launch")
print(prompt)

# Build prompts for every agent at once
all_prompts = AgentPromptBuilder.build_all_prompts("My idea", "basic")

docs.title: Credentials (optional)
docs.text:
If you keep agent keys in a local .env, Mocky can read them to label which agents are configured:

config = AgentPromptBuilder.load_agent_config(".env")
expected = AgentPromptBuilder.get_expected_env_keys()  # agent -> env var name

Never commit secrets

Copy .env.example to .env and fill in only the keys you use. .env is git-ignored by default. Mocky reads these keys locally and never transmits them.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thanks for your interest in improving Mocky! The full guide lives in CONTRIBUTING.md. Here's the short version.

docs.title: Dev setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/raman0c17/mocky.git
cd mocky
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Before you open a PR
docs.text:
ruff check .
ruff format --check .
pytest
  • Keep changes cross-platform — no OS-specific dependencies on the default path.
  • Follow Conventional Commits for commit messages.
  • Add or update tests where it makes sense.

docs.title: Good first issues
docs.text:
• Add a new template to the template library.
  • Add a new agent to the prompt builder.
  • Improve these docs.

docs.title: Code of Conduct
docs.text: This project follows the Contributor Covenant.

docs.title: Platform-agnostic by design
docs.text:
Mocky's rendering engine is built on python-pptx, which is pure Python. That means:

  • No Microsoft PowerPoint or Office install required.
  • No Windows-only COM (pywin32 / comtypes) dependency.
  • Identical behavior on Windows (x64/ARM), macOS (Intel/Apple Silicon), and Linux.

The public API is stable across platforms:

from mocky import PowerPointGenerator
PowerPointGenerator().create_presentation(slides, "out.pptx")

docs.title: Use it as a library
docs.text:
Mocky is a normal installable package:

pip install mocky

from mocky import (
    MarkdownParser,
    PowerPointGenerator,
    TemplateLibrary,
    AgentPromptBuilder,
    FileManager,
)

docs.title: Run it in a container
docs.text:
A slim image is provided (python:3.12-slim, non-root user, no Office needed):

# Build
docker build -t mocky .

# Run, mounting your input/output folders
docker run --rm -it \
  -v "$PWD/input_files:/app/input_files" \
  -v "$PWD/presentations:/app/presentations" \
  mocky

Multi-arch images

Because the stack is pure Python, the same Dockerfile builds for both linux/amd64 and linux/arm64 — e.g. with docker buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64.

docs.title: Do I need Microsoft PowerPoint or Office installed?
docs.text: No. Mocky renders with python-pptx, which is pure Python. Nothing from Microsoft Office is required on any platform.

docs.title: Which operating systems are supported?
docs.text: Windows (x64 and ARM), macOS (Intel and Apple Silicon), and Linux. The same code path runs everywhere.

docs.title: I got error: externally-managed-environment when installing.
docs.text:
That's a macOS/Homebrew (PEP 668) guard. Install inside a virtual environment:

python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"

Avoid --break-system-packages, which can damage your Homebrew Python.

docs.title: Does Mocky call AI services for me?
docs.text: No. The agent prompt builder only generates prompt text for you to paste into the agent of your choice. Your credentials never leave your machine.

docs.title: Where do generated files go?
docs.text:
• Scaffolded Markdown → generated_markdowns/
  • Agent prompt bundles → prompt_outputs/
  • Rendered decks → presentations/

All of these are git-ignored by default.

docs.title: How do I report a bug or request a feature?
docs.text: Open an issue using the templates on the GitHub repository.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.9 or newer
  • Works on Windows (x64/ARM), macOS (Intel/Apple Silicon), and Linux

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
From PyPIFrom source 
pip install mocky

git clone https://github.com/raman0c17/mocky.git
cd mocky
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate      # Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"

externally-managed-environment error?

On macOS/Homebrew Python, install inside a virtual environment (the python3 -m venv .venv step above). Avoid --break-system-packages.

docs.title: Your first deck
docs.text:
mocky

Choose "Generate a presentation from an idea", type your idea, pick a template, and Mocky writes a .pptx into presentations/.

docs.title: Verify your install
docs.text:
python -c "import mocky; print(mocky.__version__)"

Next: see Usage for the CLI and library workflows.

docs.title: Claude skill
docs.text: Mocky ships a Claude skill so Claude can scaffold, refine, and render decks for you. It's a community give-back: anyone using Claude can drop the skill in and get Mocky's idea→deck flow.

docs.title: What the skill does
docs.text:
• Recognizes requests like “make a presentation about X” or “turn this Markdown into PowerPoint.”
  • Chooses a workflow: scaffold from an idea, or convert existing Markdown.
  • Renders a real .pptx with the cross-platform engine.

docs.title: Where it lives
docs.text:
skills/
└── mocky/
    └── SKILL.md

docs.title: Using it
docs.text:
Point your Claude environment at the skills/mocky/SKILL.md file (or copy it into your skills directory). The skill's front matter describes when Claude should trigger it; the body documents the slide model and both workflows.

Other coding agents

The repository also ships an AGENTS.md at its root so tools like Codex and Cursor can navigate the codebase.

docs.title: Templates
docs.text: The template library turns a one-line idea into a structured Markdown outline you can render directly or refine first.

docs.title: Built-in templates
docs.text: Template Best for Sections basic General-purpose decks Overview, Why It Matters, Key Points, Next Steps, Summary problem-solution Pitches and proposals Problem, Solution, Benefits, Roadmap, Next Steps product-launch Launch narratives Market Opportunity, Features, Go-To-Market, Success Metrics, Launch Plan

docs.title: API
docs.text:
from mocky import TemplateLibrary

TemplateLibrary.list_templates()
# ['basic', 'problem-solution', 'product-launch']

markdown = TemplateLibrary.build_markdown("My idea", "problem-solution")
TemplateLibrary.save_markdown(markdown, "generated_markdowns", "my_idea.md")

build_markdown accepts an optional additional_fields dict to override any section's default text:

markdown = TemplateLibrary.build_markdown(
    "My idea",
    "basic",
    additional_fields={"overview": "A custom overview paragraph."},
)

docs.title: Adding a template
docs.text: Templates are plain dictionaries in src/mocky/template_lib.py. Add an entry to TEMPLATES with a name, description, and a list of sections (each section is a Markdown string with {placeholder} fields), then send a PR. New templates are a great first contribution!

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: Mocky can be driven two ways: the interactive CLI or the Python library.

docs.title: The slide model
docs.text:
A deck is a list of slide dicts:

slides = [
    {"title": "Slide title", "content": ["A bullet", "Another bullet"]},
    {"title": "With a picture", "content": [{"image": "/path/to/local.png"}]},
    {"title": "With a link", "content": [{"link": "https://example.com"}]},
]

Markdown maps to this model as: the first # is the deck title, every ## starts a new slide, and paragraphs / lists / images / links become content items.

docs.title: CLI
docs.text:
mocky

You'll get a menu:

  1. Generate from an idea — pick a template, Mocky scaffolds Markdown into generated_markdowns/, writes agent prompts into prompt_outputs/, and renders a .pptx into presentations/.
  2. Convert an existing Markdown file — drop a .md file in input_files/ and convert it directly.

docs.title: Workflow A — from an idea
docs.text:
from mocky import TemplateLibrary, MarkdownParser, PowerPointGenerator

idea = "Launching Mocky 1.0 to the open-source community"
markdown = TemplateLibrary.build_markdown(idea, "product-launch")
path = TemplateLibrary.save_markdown(markdown, "generated_markdowns", "launch.md")

parsed = MarkdownParser().parse_markdown(path)
PowerPointGenerator().create_presentation(parsed["slides"], "presentations/launch.pptx")

docs.title: Workflow B — from existing Markdown
docs.text:
from mocky import MarkdownParser, PowerPointGenerator

parsed = MarkdownParser().parse_markdown("input_files/my_outline.md")
PowerPointGenerator().create_presentation(parsed["slides"], "presentations/out.pptx")

docs.title: Build slides yourself
docs.text:
from mocky import PowerPointGenerator

slides = [
    {"title": "Hello", "content": ["First bullet", "Second bullet"]},
    {"title": "Thanks", "content": []},
]
PowerPointGenerator().create_presentation(slides, "presentations/manual.pptx")

Images

Mocky only fetches http(s) image URLs (with a timeout); other schemes are skipped. Local image paths must exist on disk or they're ignored.
